responses.content: C. “There is no reliable record of exactly when or how it happened, but at some point, Hatshepsut took a bold and unprecedented step: She had herself crowned pharaoh with the large, heavy, red-and-white double crown of the two Egypts, north and south.”

questions.question: The author creates a contrast in the opening sentences between what Hatshepsut didn't do and what she did do. What does this contrast reveal about her values as a leader?

responses.content: (17) With the exception of one military campaign against Nubia, Hatshepsut’s reign was peaceful. Instead of expanding Egypt’s borders through war and conquest, Hatshepsut built monuments within her country to proclaim its power. Her masterpiece was the magnificent temple at the site known today as Deir el-Bahri. The temple was dedicated to Amen, the god who was supposed to be the divine father of every pharaoh, the god to whom Hatshepsut felt she owed her good fortune. The temple at Deir el-Bahri was said to be Hatshepsut’s own mortuary temple. The building is set into the side of a mountain and rises gracefully in three beautifully proportioned tiers, each supported by columns like those to be seen centuries later in Greek temples. Its design was far ahead of its time. Hatshepsut called it Djeser-Djeseru (JEH-sir jeh-SEH-roo)—“Holy of Holies.”In your ownwords

questions.question: How does Malala know the school is closing permanently?

responses.content: We had a special assembly that final morning, but it was hard to hear with the noise of helicopters overhead. Some of us spoke out against what was happening in our valley. The bell rang for the very last time, and then Madam Maryam announced it was winter vacation. But unlike in other years no date was announced for the start of next term. Even so, some teachers still gave us homework. In the yard I hugged all my friends. I looked at the honors board and wondered if my name would ever appear on it again. Exams were due in March, but how could they take place? Coming first didn’t matter if you couldn’t study at all. When someone takes away your pens you realize quite how important education is. The bell rang for the very last time, and then Madam Maryam announced it was winter vacation. But unlike in other years no date was announced for the start of next term. Even so, some teachers still gave us homework. In the yard I hugged all my friends. I looked at the honors board and wondered if my name would ever appear on it again. Exams were due in March, but how could they take place? Coming first didn’t matter if you couldn’t study at all. When someone takes away your pens you realize quite how important education is.

The response demonstrates text-specific engagement but misses the specific threshold for 'correct.'

questions.question: Based on the text, why does the Taliban condemn the education of girls and not the education of boys?

The answer is in the student's own words and demonstrates understanding of the text's key points.
